channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Xbox Game Pass For China Seemingly in Development
item.content:encoded:
New findings suggest that Microsoft may be developing a Chinese version of Xbox Game Pass.

Since the arrival of the new CEO, Asha Sharma, Xbox Game Pass has seen several changes, including lower prices, a new Starter tier, and more. Now, it seems the console maker could be working to extend these services for players in China; however, this hasn’t been officially confirmed by the company.

Xbox PC App Update Hints At Game Pass for China

According to a report by The Verge, the Xbox PC app’s latest update points towards Xbox Game Pass coming to China. Within the files, a mention of ‘Project Saluki’ has been found, and the description for this codename states, “China market expansion for Game Pass, Rewards, and subscription tiers.” Other than that, no other details were revealed.

The report adds that Microsoft has been selling consoles in China for years and selling games through Activision Blizzard. However, many games are not always approved by regulators, which is a problem publishers often face when trying to distribute their games in the region.

The same challenge applies to Xbox Game Pass, as not all games offered through the service may be approved in China. However, they could opt for a smaller library of games, with a new tier specifically catered to China. At the moment, this is just speculation.

Xbox PC App Update Also Mentions Codename Positron

Along with Project Saluki, the Xbox PC App update also mentioned ‘Positron,’ which is speculated to “enable Disc2Digital.” Currently, this is just speculation, but players think that through Positron, Xbox could introduce digital licenses through disc games. This is something the company had planned to do before, but decided to back away from it due to backlash from publishers and resellers.

In other news, Discord has announced a new Nitro Rewards program that includes an Xbox Game Pass membership and major product savings. Xbox Game Pass brings a stacked lineup for May 2026 Wave 1, with Forza Horizon 6 headlining the collection. What are your thoughts on Xbox Game Pass coming to China? Leave your thoughts down in the comments, and join the official Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more information from Insider Gaming, read about Marvel Tokon’s story mode, mostly featuring motion comics. Don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.

item.title: PGL Astana 2026 Playoff Preview: Who Will Take the Trophy?
item.content:encoded:
PGL Astana 2026 moves on to its playoff stage at the Barys Arena following the conclusion of the Swiss Stage, with eight teams advancing to the playoffs. The last day of the Swiss Stage saw magic, G2, and Aurora complete the playoff bracket.

The playoff bracket for PGL Astana 2026 is:

  • MOUZ vs Aurora
  • Team Spirit vs G2
  • 9z vs magic
  • Furia vs Falcons

Spirit, Furia, MOUZ, and karrigan’s Falcons advance to the playoffs

Insider Gaming’s tournament guide for PGL Astana pointed to Team Spirit as the favourites for the event, and there is no reason to think otherwise after the Swiss Stage.

The Russian roster proceeded through the Swiss Stage with relative ease, dropping only one map across the three series, losing Overpass in overtime to Furia.

The team is by no means flawless in a general sense, expressing to the media that they are struggling to balance individual vs team play without their coach, Sergey ‘hally’ Shavaev. Yet, that balance had looked better since IEM Rio, making them favourites without Vitality present.

While Spirit’s 3-0 record was predictable, 9z’s 3-0 Swiss run was anything but. Wins against Parivision, Falcons, and MOUZ completed a legit showing from the South American roster, with strong showings from Luciano ‘luchov’ Herrera and Franco ‘dgt’ Garcia

Furia recovered from a last-place finish at BLAST Rivals Fort Worth to make the playoffs with a 3-1 record, finding comfort in Overpass yet again. The roster had specialized in the map at times last year, giving them an edge in their rivalry with Vitality, but it had since collapsed.

The rediscovery of both the map and Danil ‘molodoy’ Golubenko’s form will give fans hope that they can recapture their 2025 ways, but as with IEM Rio, the proof will be in the playoff pudding.

MOUZ also joined the playoffs with a 3-1 record in Dorian ‘xertioN’ Berman’s IGLing debut, combined with Adrian ‘xelex’ Vincze and Justinas ‘jL’ Lekavicius’s first showings.

MOUZ’s progress has largely been the xertioN show, with the entry IGL delivering huge impact, but can the player continue to have that impact in the playoffs? Historically, that hasn’t been the case.

Falcons advance to their first playoffs since the addition of Finn ‘karrigan’ Andersen in spite of the loss to 9z. Falcons by no means showed complete CS in the groups, winning messy brawls against k27, Monte, and The MongolZ.

Nevertheless, karrigan has always shown his best work in the arena, and if the vibes are good within the team, we might see that magic again in Astana.

magic will definitely be there, in fact, as hometown hero Abdurakhim ‘mo0N’ Issa and his team earned a spot in the playoffs in a surprising run which saw the team recover from 0-2 to 3-2 in the Swiss.

G2 joined the playoffs alongside magic on the last day of the Swiss after an unconvincing run. The roster is, in a sense, relatively new after the addition of Guy ‘NertZ’ Iluz, but their issues are not.

Aurora were the last team to qualify after a barnstormer series against The MongolZ, which saw the roster fight back on Overpass, before a Mirage that seemed to be slipping out of their hands, before a 2HP clutch from Ismailcan ‘XANTARES’ Dörtkardeş.

Parivision and The MongolZ miss out

Parivision was a team to watch at PGL Astana. The roster is one of the few teams to win a Tier 1 event, with their BLAST Bounty victory in January, but their PGL Bucharest exit showed that the roster’s high floor may be collapsing.

Parivision coach, Dastan ‘dastan’ Akbayev, had explicitly stated that the event was a goal for the roster, with the Kazakhstani coach hoping to play in the arena in front of his home crowd, but they could only guarantee a group stage exit.

Following elimination after losses to 9z, Aurora, and G2, dastan openly discussed the concept of changing the roster’s riflers on broadcast, looking at the young guns who had seemingly stepped up to Tier 1 seamlessly in January, but are now not adapting to the scrutiny that Tier 1 teams put their opponents under.

The coach put Andrey ‘BELCHONOKK’ Yasinskiy and Emil ‘nota’ Moskvitin in the spotlight in that broadcast interview, having previously liked a Telegram post from a fan who stated that nota should be kicked.

The major issue with The MongolZ in their current form is that they never seem to be in control of any particular match, or more accurately, never keep control.

The roster, at times, had Spirit, Falcons, and Aurora on the ropes, but their uncompromising playstyle means that each round is a roll of the dice in terms of their own aggression working or not. At PGL Astana, they were just shy of that balance going in their favour, but to no avail.

Who do you think will win PGL Astana 2026? Let us know on our Discord!
For other CS news, see our catch-up on IEM Atlanta 2026.

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: IEM Atlanta 2026: Vitality Shocked by BetBoom, BC Game Eliminated, and NA Hopes Fade
item.content:encoded:
IEM Atlanta 2026 saw Team Vitality suffer an unexpected Group Stage loss to BetBoom as the tournament moves steadily towards the playoffs.

The results so far mean that four of the six playoff spots are decided, with BetBoom, paiN, NAVI, and Legacy guaranteed to take the stage at the Georgia World Congress Center.

The event also said goodbye to BC.Game, NRG, Passion UA, and M80, with Oleksandr ‘s1mple’ Kostyliev and the majority of the North American players leaving the event.

Rough Vitality suffers BetBoom defeat

Vitality were stunned by BetBoom at IEM Atlanta, with the Russian org providing a distinctly proactive CT side which didn’t let Vitality breathe on Anubis.

The map was a strong showing of counter-stratting, with BetBoom choosing to make every round, win or lose, due to their own decision-making, resulting in a 13-10 victory on the map.

Moving on to Overpass, Vitality comprehensively dismantled BetBoom on a weak map for Kirill ‘Boombl4’ Mikhailov’s men, with Vitality boasting a commanding 9-3 T side before converting the map 13-8.

Moving on to Nuke, Vitality showed a rare crack with their T-sides on Nuke being in question. The hard side of the map, Vitality delivered only one round on their T side against NAVI in Fort Worth, and now two rounds against BetBoom in Atlanta.

Unlike NAVI, BetBoom recovered from Vitality’s forcebuy win to eke out the win 13-8 with Kirill ‘Magnojex’ Rodnov shining with a 20/8/1 KDA.

There is no reason to panic for Vitality just yet, though they will face decent tests against FaZe followed by the winners of B8/FUT if they progress.

First of all, Vitality continues to look like different beats once they enter the playoffs. Their IEM Rio loss to Falcons seemingly had no bearing on their playoff dominance.

Additionally, Vitality has been open about their lack of practice since IEM Rio, prioritizing their energy for the IEM Cologne 2026 Major.

Instead, the Vitality contingent has been seen galivanting through America’s ‘Great Outdoors,’ with Robin ‘ropz’ Kool, Shahar ‘flameZ’ Shushan, and Mathieu ‘ZywOo’ Herbaut sharing a video of themselves chanting “f**k you, apEX” at Yosemite National Park.

IEM Atlanta sees BC.Game and NA teams struggle again

BC.Game was once again eliminated in last place of a tier 1 event, this time with their trial analyst, Robin ‘ScrunK Röpke, standing in. The event was another disaster for BC.Game, with roster disruption adding to the questionable nature of the project.

The event did, however, once again show that s1mple could still get it done under the right conditions, delivering an electrifying 28/13/4 KDA against Vitality on Dust 2 in the 0-2 series loss.

Leaving alongside BC.Game in last place were NRG, M80, and Passion UA, removing the majority of NA players at the NA event.

NRG fell to FaZe in a competitive 0-2 loss, with Passion UA losing to the Major-qualified Sinners roster 1-2, resulting in PUA’s last NA piece, Nick ‘nicx’ Lee, leaving the competition.

Liquid were perhaps only saved by having to face a team with more NA representation in the lower bracket, as they secured their first competitive victory since March.

You can discuss the IEM Atlanta 2026 on our Discord!
For more CS, see our the CS Asia Championships groups!

Darragh is an Esports Journalist for Insider Gaming specialising in Counter-Strike. He loves to explore how esports teams work, or why they very often do not.

More from Darragh Harbinson
